originalTitle: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
title: Friends in Africa: Talking About Problems to Find Peace!
content:
Hello, super kids! It’s your friendly African news reporter here, ready to tell you a story about two amazing countries right here on our wonderful continent. Sometimes, even grown-ups and whole countries have problems, just like you might have with a friend. But guess what? They are trying to fix them! Today, we’re talking about two neighbours: a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DRC for short, and another country called Rwanda. They are both beautiful places in Africa, and they are trying to be good friends again.

Who are these neighbours?

Imagine two big houses next to each other in a huge village. That’s a bit like DRC and Rwanda! They are two very special countries in Africa, sharing a border. This means they are right next to each other on the map. DRC is a very, very big country, with lots of forests, big rivers, and amazing animals like gorillas and okapis. Rwanda is a smaller country, but it is just as beautiful! It’s often called the "Land of a Thousand Hills" because it has so many lovely green hills. Both countries are full of friendly people and exciting cultures. They want to live in peace and help each other grow. Did you know? The Democratic Republic of Congo is the second-largest country in Africa! It’s so big you could fit many other countries inside it!

What's the Big Talk About?

So, these two neighbours, DRC and Rwanda, have had some big disagreements for a very, very long time. It's like when friends don't agree on how to play a game, but on a much, much bigger scale! These problems have caused sadness and sometimes even made it hard for people to live peacefully in some parts. When countries have problems, it's called a 'conflict'. A conflict means they are not agreeing and sometimes it can lead to bigger issues. But now, something important is happening. They are going to a very special place to talk things over and try to make things right. They want to find a way to stop the problems and make sure everyone can be safe and happy. It's a big step towards being good neighbours.

A Very Sad Time

A long, long time ago, in 1994, something very sad happened in Rwanda. It was a time called the Rwanda genocide. During this sad time, many, many people were hurt and lost their lives. It was a very, very difficult and heartbreaking event for everyone. After this very difficult period, things became even harder between Rwanda and its neighbours, including DRC. Many problems started to happen between them. The Democratic Republic of Congo says that a lot of the problems they have today started after that sad time. They want to talk about these problems so that everyone can live in peace now and in the future. It’s about understanding the past to build a better tomorrow. Did you know? Even after sad times, people work very hard to heal and build a better future, full of hope and kindness!

Going to a Special Judge!

Where do countries go when they have big problems they can’t fix alone? They go to a super-special judge! This judge is not for people like you and me, but for whole countries! It's like a big grown-up who helps friends work out their problems, but for entire nations. This special place is called the International Court of Justice, or ICJ. You can think of it as a giant meeting room where countries can tell their stories and ask for help to find a fair solution. It’s a very important place where big decisions are made to help keep the world peaceful. Did you know? The International Court of Justice is in a far-off country called the Netherlands, in a city called The Hague! That’s a long journey from Africa!

Why is DRC Talking to the Judge?

The Democratic Republic of Congo is going to this special court to tell the judges all about the problems they have had with Rwanda. DRC says that Rwanda has done some things that are not fair or right, and it has caused trouble in their country. They believe rules have been broken. They want the judges to listen carefully and help them find a way to stop these problems from happening again. They hope the court can help them find justice and peace. They want to make sure that everyone, especially the children, can live safely and happily without any more trouble.

What Happens Next?

Now that DRC has told its story, the special judges at the ICJ will listen very carefully. They will also listen to Rwanda, who will share its side of the story. The judges need to hear from both countries to understand everything. Their job is to understand all the problems and then help both countries find a good and fair way to solve them. It’s like when a teacher helps two friends understand each other and find a way to share their toys or play together nicely. It takes time, but it’s a big step towards peace. Did you know? Judges at the International Court of Justice come from many different countries around the world! They are very smart people who know a lot about laws and fairness.

Hopes for a Peaceful Future

Everyone in Africa, and all around the world, wants DRC and Rwanda to become good neighbours and friends again. When countries get along, they can help each other, trade things, and build a better future for all their children. It’s like when you and your friends work together to build a cool sandcastle! When there are no fights, kids can go to school every day, play outside safely, and grow up in a happy home. They can learn and dream big dreams without any worries. That’s what we all hope for! So, there you have it, my little news adventurers! Even when there are big problems, countries like the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda are trying their best to talk and find peace. It shows that even big neighbours can learn to solve their disagreements with a little help and a lot of hope. Let's all wish them well on their journey to peace and friendship!
summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Rwanda, two neighbouring countries in Africa, are asking a special international court to help them solve long-standing problems and find a peaceful way to be good friends.
whyMatters: It's important because when countries are friends and work together, everyone can live happily and safely. This means children can go to school, play, and grow up without worrying about big problems, making Africa a better place for everyone.
